assets.name: lecture_02_figure_01.png
assets.rationale: None of the candidate frames are useful for note writing. They are all title-card bumper shots rather than lecture content, with no blackboard mathematics, diagram, or board layout to preserve. Candidate 03 is the most complete and settled version of the bumper, but the whole subtitle window should be rejected for the chapter body.
assets.subtitle_excerpt: This program is brought to you by Stanford University.

assets.name: lecture_02_figure_02.png
assets.subtitle_excerpt: components of the state of the equation are not the same
assets.caption_hint: components of the state of the equation are not the same

assets.name: lecture_02_figure_03.png
assets.rationale: This frame gives the clearest and most complete view of the board at the moment the lecture introduces the linear vector space of states. Susskind has moved far enough to the right that the central coefficient-times-ket expressions and their associated two-component column vectors are least occluded, while the left-hand resulting state vector is still visible. Earlier frames hide more of the central equations behind the lecturer.

assets.name: lecture_02_figure_04.png
assets.rationale: This frame gives the clearest and most settled view of the board for the identification of spin-up and spin-down with basis vectors. Susskind has moved far enough away that the left-hand column-vector expressions and their labeling are least occluded, and the equality relating the labels to the vectors is more readable than in the earlier frames, where his hand and body block more of the notation.
assets.subtitle_excerpt: of vectors labeling up state and down state.
assets.caption_hint: Up and down states as basis vectors
